title: Cloud-Native Attack Methodology
tools_required: AWS CLI, awscli, AWS Lambda tools
when_to_use: Use Cloud-Native Attack Methodology to develop professional-grade cloud-native attack methodology capabilities at Mastery level. This recipe is part of the Specialist Domains pillar and integrates with linked missions OM-M09, OM-M10.

steps.title: Cloud Architecture Enumeration
steps.instruction: Comprehensive enumeration of cloud-native architecture: map all functions (AWS Lambda, Azure Functions), APIs (API Gateway, OpenAPI specs), containers (ECS/EKS/GKE), databases (RDS, DynamoDB, Cosmos), IAM roles and policies. Document: exposure surface (publicly accessible endpoints), authentication mechanisms, privilege levels. Use ScoutSuite or CloudMapper for visualization.
steps.tip: Cloud enumeration requires valid credentials (from initial compromise). Use IAM user privileges to scope enumeration.
steps.expected_output: Architecture map with attack surface inventory

steps.title: Serverless Exploitation
steps.instruction: Exploit serverless functions: Lambda injection attacks (execute arbitrary Python/Node code), environment variable harvesting (steal secrets, API keys, credentials), privilege escalation via overpermissioned IAM roles. Demonstrate: break out of Lambda sandbox, enumerate other functions, access RDS/S3. Chain to gain persistent access.
steps.tip: Lambda functions are stateless but have temporary credentials; extract and reuse them.

steps.title: CI/CD Pipeline Compromise
steps.instruction: Compromise CI/CD pipelines: GitHub Actions workflow injection (add malicious steps), secret extraction from workflow logs, artifact manipulation (inject backdoors into build outputs), supply chain attack (poison dependencies). Demonstrate: workflow modification, secret exfiltration, package publication with backdoor.
steps.tip: CI/CD pipelines have powerful privileges; compromise here impacts all downstream users.
steps.expected_output: CI/CD compromise with supply chain impact

steps.title: Container Environment Assessment
steps.instruction: Analyze ECS task definitions and IAM roles: excessive permissions, secrets in environment variables, shared task roles, container escape paths. Attack container: privilege escalation, lateral movement to other containers, compromise underlying EC2 instance. Demonstrate: container breakout, node compromise, cluster dominance.
steps.tip: Container security is layered: container isolation + host security + orchestration policies.

steps.title: Cross-Service Attack Chain
steps.instruction: Chain exploits across services for maximum impact: compromise Lambda -> harvest credentials -> pivot to RDS -> extract customer data -> modify CI/CD -> persist backdoor. Each step provides access to higher-value targets. Document: privilege escalation path, data exposure potential, persistence mechanisms.
steps.tip: Cloud security is only as strong as the weakest service. One misconfigured Lambda can compromise entire organization.

steps.title: Cloud-Native Security Assessment Report
steps.instruction: Comprehensive assessment (15+ pages): architecture review, enumerated services, exploitation results, privilege escalation chains, data exposure, persistence capabilities. Include: remediation guidance (least privilege, secrets rotation, monitoring), architectural recommendations (service segmentation, API gateways, WAF). Compare security posture to cloud provider best practices.
steps.tip: Cloud reports must address shared responsibility model: what client controls vs what provider controls.
